heading: Adverse-event reporting
label: 355b Adverse-event reporting

children.text: The rule shall provide for the implementation of such labeling requirement in a manner that the Secretary considers to be most likely to reach the broadest consumer audience.
In promulgating the rule, the Secretary shall seek to minimize the cost of the rule on the pharmacy profession.
The rule shall take effect not later than 60 days after the date on which the rule is promulgated.

children.heading: Drugs with pediatric market exclusivity
children.label: b Drugs with pediatric market exclusivity

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: During the one year beginning on the date on which a drug receives a period of market exclusivity under 505A of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act [], any report of an adverse event regarding the drug that the Secretary of Health and Human Services receives shall be referred to the Office of Pediatric Therapeutics established under . In considering the report, the Director of such Office shall provide for the review of the report by the Pediatric Advisory Committee, including obtaining any recommendations of such subcommittee regarding whether the Secretary should take action under the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act [ et seq.] in response to the report.121 U.S.C. 355asection 393a of this title221 U.S.C. 301So in original. Probably should be preceded by “section”.1So in original. Probably should be “Committee”.2

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 2 Rule of construction
children.text: Paragraph (1) may not be construed as restricting the authority of the Secretary of Health and Human Services to continue carrying out the activities described in such paragraph regarding a drug after the one-year period described in such paragraph regarding the drug has expired.
